checkin.title: Daily check-in
checkin.question: Did you have a headache today?
checkin.duration: How long did the headache last?
checkin.symptoms: Did you notice any of these?
checkin.medicine: Did you take medicine for the headache?
checkin.activity: How much did the headache limit your usual activity?
checkin.notes: Anything else you want to note?
checkin.notesPlaceholder: e.g. skipped a meal, long travel…
checkin.sleep: How was your sleep last night?
checkin.stress: How stressed did you feel today?
checkin.alreadyLoggedHint: You can change it until midnight.

summary.title: Summary
summary.notesEmpty: Keep logging daily — your notes will appear here.
summary.noData: No entries yet. Start with today's check-in.

diary.title: Diary
diary.empty: No entries yet. Your daily records will appear here.

settings.title: Settings
settings.reminderBody: One reminder each day at the time you choose.
settings.batteryBody: Some phones stop app reminders to save battery. Open your phone Settings → Battery → find HeadTrack Nepal and allow background activity (on Xiaomi/Redmi choose "No restrictions"; on Samsung turn off "Put app to sleep"; on Oppo/Vivo allow "Auto-start").
settings.wellbeingBody: A short 9-question wellbeing check, done at the start of the study and again at 8 weeks with the study team.
settings.clinicSummaryBody: Creates a summary of your diary on this phone to show at your next visit. Nothing is sent anywhere.
settings.researchExportBody: Creates a de-identified, passphrase-protected file for the study team. Only your study ID is included — never your name.
settings.privacyTitle: Your data stays on this phone
settings.privacyBody: Everything you record is stored only on this phone, in an encrypted database. The app never connects to the internet, and there is no account or cloud. Your records are identified by your study ID, not your name.
settings.disclaimerBody: This app is a diary. It does not make a diagnosis, does not recommend or change medicines, and does not replace assessment by a clinician. If you have a sudden very severe headache, a headache with fever and stiff neck, weakness of the face or limbs, confusion, or the worst headache of your life, seek medical care immediately — do not wait for the app.

rules.mouBody: You used acute headache medicine frequently this month — on {{count}} of the last 28 days. Please discuss this pattern with your clinician.
rules.sleepBody: You recorded headache on several days after poor sleep. This is an association in your diary and does not prove causation.
rules.burdenBody: Your headache burden has increased compared with the previous period ({{current}} headache days this week vs {{previous}} last week). Please arrange a clinical review.
rules.tipBody: Regular sleep, meals, hydration, and prescribed treatment may support headache management.

phq9.title: Wellbeing check (PHQ-9)
phq9.intro: Over the last 2 weeks, how often have you been bothered by any of the following problems?
items: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
Feeling tired or having little energy
Feeling bad about yourself — or that you are a failure or have let yourself or your family down
Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed? Or the opposite — being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way
phq9.safetyBody: One of your answers suggests you may have had thoughts of self-harm. Please contact the study clinician or the health centre now — this is important and you will be supported. If you are in immediate danger, go to the nearest health facility.
phq9.safetyContact: Contact: your study clinician at Sunthan Primary Health Centre

onboarding.welcomeBody: A simple daily headache diary for the HeadTrack Nepal study. One question a day; a few more only on headache days.
onboarding.enrolBody: To be completed with the study team.
onboarding.historyMonths: How long have you had recurrent headaches? (months)
onboarding.reminderBody: Choose a time for your daily check-in reminder. You can change it later in Settings.
onboarding.batteryTitle: One more step for reliable reminders
onboarding.finishBody: Log your first entry from the Today tab. Thank you for taking part in the study.

reminder.notificationBody: How was your day? Log today's entry — it takes less than a minute.

export.passBody: Set a passphrase (at least 8 characters). The study team needs this passphrase to open the file — share it separately from the file.
